{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "95be11dc",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# RasterIO \n",
    "## A brief intro to a *pythonic* raster library\n",
    "\n",
    "In order to run this notebook, please do what follows:\n",
    "\n",
    "    cd  /media/sf_LVM_shared/my_SE_data/exercise\n",
    "    jupyter-notebook RasterIO_Intro.ipynb\n",
    "\n",
    "---\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "4de3e828",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "**RasterIO** is a library that simplifies the use of the raster swiss-knife geospatial library (**GDAL**) from Python (... and talking about that, [how do you pronounce GDAL?](https://soundcloud.com/danabauer/how-do-you-pronounce-gdal)).\n",
    "\n",
    "Ratio: GDAL is C++ library with a public C interface for other languages bindings, therefore its Python API is quite rough from the mean Python programmer's point of view (i.e. it is not *pythonic* enough). RasterIO is for rasters what Fiona is for vectors.\n",
    "\n",
    "*Note: [`Open Source Approaches in Spatial Data Handling`](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-3-540-74831-1) is a good comprehensive book about the history and roles of FOSS in the geospatial domain.*\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "56ff4040",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Rasterio basics"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "7e6618bc",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import rasterio # that's to use it"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "f9cce0fd",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "'1.3.2'"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 3,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "rasterio.__version__"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 38,
   "id": "4bdee418",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "dataset = rasterio.open('geodata/dem/SA_elevation_mn_GMTED2010_mn_msk.tif', mode='r') # r+ w w+ r"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "a1a061c0",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "A lots of different parameters can be specified, including all GDAL valid open() attributes. Most of the parameters make sense in write mode to create a new raster file:\n",
    "* dtype\n",
    "* nodata\n",
    "* crs\n",
    "* width, height\n",
    "* transform\n",
    "* count\n",
    "* driver\n",
    "\n",
    "More information are available in the API reference documentation [here](https://rasterio.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api/rasterio.html#rasterio.open). Note that in some specific cases you will need to preceed a regular `open()` with `rasterio.Env()` to customize `GDAL` for your uses (e.g. caching, color tables, driver options, etc.).\n",
    "\n",
    "Note that the filename in general can be any valid URL and even a dataset within some container file (e.g. a variable in Netcdf or HDF5 file). Even object storage (e.g AWS S3) and zip URLs can be considered."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "a2ee4555",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Let's see a series of simple methods that can be used to access metadata and pixel values"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 39,
   "id": "912133af",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "<open DatasetReader name='geodata/dem/SA_elevation_mn_GMTED2010_mn_msk.tif' mode='r'>"
      ]

     "execution_count": 39,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }

  },

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",

   "source": [

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "a1273eaa",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Rasterio includes also some utility methods to show raster data, using `matplotlib` under the hood."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 61,
   "id": "be57ba58",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import rasterio.plot"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 67,
   "id": "035f39ae",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "\n",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 432x288 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {
      "needs_background": "light"
     },
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "<AxesSubplot:title={'center':'Elevation'}>"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 67,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "rasterio.plot.show(band, title='Elevation')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 63,
   "id": "735f6b98",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "(-83.0, -34.0, -56.0, 14.0)"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 63,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "rasterio.plot.plotting_extent(dataset)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 64,
   "id": "074cfd84",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "\n",

      ]
     },
     "metadata": {
      "needs_background": "light"
     },
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "rasterio.plot.show_hist(band, bins=100)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "7b5e197f",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Note that you need to explicity filter out nodata values at read time."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 65,
   "id": "ab69dc1a",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "masked_array(\n",
       "  data=[[[--, --, --, ..., --, --, --],\n",
       "         [--, --, --, ..., --, --, --],\n",
       "         [--, --, --, ..., --, --, --],\n",
       "         ...,\n",
       "         [--, --, --, ..., --, --, --],\n",
       "         [--, --, --, ..., --, --, --],\n",
       "         [--, --, --, ..., --, --, --]]],\n",
       "  mask=[[[ True,  True,  True, ...,  True,  True,  True],\n",
       "         [ True,  True,  True, ...,  True,  True,  True],\n",
       "         [ True,  True,  True, ...,  True,  True,  True],\n",
       "         ...,\n",
       "         [ True,  True,  True, ...,  True,  True,  True],\n",
       "         [ True,  True,  True, ...,  True,  True,  True],\n",
       "         [ True,  True,  True, ...,  True,  True,  True]]],\n",
       "  fill_value=-9999,\n",
       "  dtype=int16)"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 65,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "band = dataset.read(masked=True)\n",
    "band"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 66,
   "id": "7b0b8e38",
   "metadata": {
    "scrolled": true
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "\n",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 432x288 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {
      "needs_background": "light"
     },
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "rasterio.plot.show_hist(band, bins=100)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "92336f1e",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "The mask operation is also efection the plot layout."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 68,
   "id": "d7419097",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "\n",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 432x288 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {
      "needs_background": "light"
     },
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "<AxesSubplot:title={'center':'Elevation'}>"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 68,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "rasterio.plot.show(band, title='Elevation')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "f7e0fbcb",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Alternatively (e.g. when raster missing nodata definition, which in practice happens quite often :-)) you need to explicitly mask on your own the input data."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 69,
   "id": "cde9f196",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "\n",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 432x288 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {
      "needs_background": "light"
     },
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "\n",
    "band = dataset.read()\n",
    "band_masked = np.ma.masked_array(band, mask=(band == -9999.0)) # mask whatever required\n",
    "band_masked\n",
    "rasterio.plot.show_hist(band_masked, bins=100)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c7b94042",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "The `rasterio` can package can also read directly multi-bands images. "
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 103,
   "id": "06d0b981",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "multi = rasterio.open('geodata/glad_ard/942_crop.tif', mode='r')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 71,
   "id": "80c2cc98",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "(8, 440, 400)"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 71,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "m = multi.read()\n",
    "m.shape"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "d8ba6869",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Note that rasterio uses bands/rows/cols order in managing images, which is not the same of other packages!"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 93,
   "id": "ced6aa85",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "\n",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 432x288 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {
      "needs_background": "light"
     },
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def pct_clip(array,pct=[2,98]):\n",
    "    array_min, array_max = np.nanpercentile(array,pct[0]), np.nanpercentile(array,pct[1])\n",
    "    clip = (array - array_min) / (array_max - array_min)\n",
    "    clip[clip>1]=1\n",
    "    clip[clip<0]=0\n",
    "    return clip\n",
    "\n",
    "with rasterio.open(\"geodata/glad_ard/942_crop.tif\") as src:\n",
    "    with rasterio.open(\n",
    "            '942_crop.tif', 'w+',\n",
    "            driver='GTiff',\n",
    "            dtype= rasterio.float32,\n",
    "            count=3,\n",
    "            crs = src.crs,\n",
    "            width=src.width,\n",
    "            height=src.height,\n",
    "            transform=src.transform,\n",
    "        ) as dst:\n",
    "        V = pct_clip(src.read(4))\n",
    "        dst.write(V,1)\n",
    "        V = pct_clip(src.read(3))\n",
    "        dst.write(V,2)\n",
    "        V = pct_clip(src.read(2))\n",
    "        dst.write(V,3)\n",
    "    \n",
    "fig,ax=plt.subplots()\n",
    "with rasterio.open(\"942_crop.tif\") as src2:\n",
    "    rasterio.plot.show(src2.read(),transform=src2.transform,ax=ax)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b7658780",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 1.2 An example of computation: NDVI"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "3ac10c0d",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "The real computation via `numpy` arrays"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 105,
   "id": "dd55e1aa",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "(0.07209653092006033, 0.851783458062008)"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 105,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "multi_msk = multi.read(masked=True)\n",
    "ndvi = (multi_msk[3]-multi_msk[2]) / (multi_msk[3]+multi_msk[2])\n",
    "ndvi.min(), ndvi.max()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 106,
   "id": "889c99de",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "(440, 400)"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 106,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "multi.shape"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "ed52a49f",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "In order to write the result all metainfo must be prepared, the easiest way is by using the input ones."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 107,
   "id": "7f90f313",
   "metadata": {
    "scrolled": false
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "{'driver': 'GTiff', 'dtype': 'uint16', 'nodata': None, 'width': 400, 'height': 440, 'count': 8, 'crs': CRS.from_epsg(4326), 'transform': Affine(0.00025, 0.0, 15.24,\n",
       "       0.0, -0.00025, 38.11), 'tiled': False, 'compress': 'deflate', 'interleave': 'pixel'}"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 107,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "multi.profile"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 108,
   "id": "3a02ee93",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "{'driver': 'GTiff', 'dtype': 'float32', 'nodata': None, 'width': 400, 'height': 440, 'count': 1, 'crs': CRS.from_epsg(4326), 'transform': Affine(0.00025, 0.0, 15.24,\n",
       "       0.0, -0.00025, 38.11), 'tiled': False, 'compress': 'deflate', 'interleave': 'pixel'}"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 108,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "profile = multi.profile\n",
    "profile.update(dtype=rasterio.float32, count=1, driver='GTiff')\n",
    "profile"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 109,
   "id": "a8b21079",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "with rasterio.open('geodata/glad_ard/942_crop_ndvi.tif', mode='w', **profile) as out:\n",
    "    out.write(ndvi.astype(rasterio.float32), 1)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 110,
   "id": "43b50e7d",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "\n",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 432x288 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {
      "needs_background": "light"
     },
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "<AxesSubplot:>"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 110,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "rasterio.plot.show(ndvi)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 111,
   "id": "85f66937",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "\n",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 432x288 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {
      "needs_background": "light"
     },
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "\n",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 432x288 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {
      "needs_background": "light"
     },
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "<AxesSubplot:>"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 111,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "\n",
    "urb = np.ma.masked_array(ndvi, mask=(ndvi >= 0.6))\n",
    "rasterio.plot.show_hist(urb)\n",
    "rasterio.plot.show(urb,cmap='Greys')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 112,
   "id": "5a34de2e",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "[0.07209653092006033,\n",
       " 0.579103729330517,\n",
       " 0.6012272128758722,\n",
       " 0.851783458062008,\n",
       " 0.11555229471805274]"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 112,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "ndvi_stats = [ndvi.min(), ndvi.mean(), np.ma.median(ndvi), ndvi.max(), ndvi.std()]\n",
    "ndvi_stats"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 113,
   "id": "457dcccc",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "[0.07209653092006033,\n",
       " 0.48462524261699785,\n",
       " 0.49912903658046365,\n",
       " 0.5999793856936714,\n",
       " 0.08450404687909031]"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 113,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "urb_stats = [urb.min(), urb.mean(), np.ma.median(urb), urb.max(), urb.std()]\n",
    "urb_stats"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "814d74f3",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "It is possibile to use a vector model for masking in rasterio, let's see how.\n",
    "First of all, we are creating a `GeoJSON` vector."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 137,
   "id": "0c7a972b",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "%%bash\n",
    "\n",
    "cat >/tmp/clip.json <<EOF\n",
    "{\n",
    "\"type\": \"FeatureCollection\",\n",
    "\"name\": \"clip\",\n",
    "\"crs\": { \"type\": \"name\", \"properties\": { \"name\": \"urn:ogc:def:crs:EPSG::32119\" } },\n",
    "\"features\": [\n",
    "{ \"type\": \"Feature\", \"properties\": { \"id\": null }, \"geometry\": { \"type\": \"Polygon\", \"coordinates\": [ [ [15.24, 38.09 ], [ 15.32, 38.09 ], [15.32, 38.00], [15.24, 38.00], [15.24, 38.09] ] ] } }\n",
    "]\n",
    "}\n",
    "EOF\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 138,
   "id": "31b9b03d",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "INFO: Open of `/tmp/clip.json'\r\n",
      "      using driver `GeoJSON' successful.\r\n",
      "\r\n",
      "Layer name: clip\r\n",
      "Geometry: Polygon\r\n",
      "Feature Count: 1\r\n",
      "Extent: (15.240000, 38.000000) - (15.320000, 38.090000)\r\n",
      "Layer SRS WKT:\r\n",
      "PROJCRS[\"NAD83 / North Carolina\",\r\n",
      "    BASEGEOGCRS[\"NAD83\",\r\n",
      "        DATUM[\"North American Datum 1983\",\r\n",
      "            ELLIPSOID[\"GRS 1980\",6378137,298.257222101,\r\n",
      "                LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1]]],\r\n",
      "        PRIMEM[\"Greenwich\",0,\r\n",
      "            ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433]],\r\n",
      "        ID[\"EPSG\",4269]],\r\n",
      "    CONVERSION[\"SPCS83 North Carolina zone (meters)\",\r\n",
      "        METHOD[\"Lambert Conic Conformal (2SP)\",\r\n",
      "            ID[\"EPSG\",9802]],\r\n",
      "        PARAMETER[\"Latitude of false origin\",33.75,\r\n",
      "            ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433],\r\n",
      "            ID[\"EPSG\",8821]],\r\n",
      "        PARAMETER[\"Longitude of false origin\",-79,\r\n",
      "            ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433],\r\n",
      "            ID[\"EPSG\",8822]],\r\n",
      "        PARAMETER[\"Latitude of 1st standard parallel\",36.1666666666667,\r\n",
      "            ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433],\r\n",
      "            ID[\"EPSG\",8823]],\r\n",
      "        PARAMETER[\"Latitude of 2nd standard parallel\",34.3333333333333,\r\n",
      "            ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433],\r\n",
      "            ID[\"EPSG\",8824]],\r\n",
      "        PARAMETER[\"Easting at false origin\",609601.22,\r\n",
      "            LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1],\r\n",
      "            ID[\"EPSG\",8826]],\r\n",
      "        PARAMETER[\"Northing at false origin\",0,\r\n",
      "            LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1],\r\n",
      "            ID[\"EPSG\",8827]]],\r\n",
      "    CS[Cartesian,2],\r\n",
      "        AXIS[\"easting (X)\",east,\r\n",
      "            ORDER[1],\r\n",
      "            LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1]],\r\n",
      "        AXIS[\"northing (Y)\",north,\r\n",
      "            ORDER[2],\r\n",
      "            LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1]],\r\n",
      "    USAGE[\r\n",
      "        SCOPE[\"Engineering survey, topographic mapping.\"],\r\n",
      "        AREA[\"United States (USA) - North Carolina - counties of Alamance; Alexander; Alleghany; Anson; Ashe; Avery; Beaufort; Bertie; Bladen; Brunswick; Buncombe; Burke; Cabarrus; Caldwell; Camden; Carteret; Caswell; Catawba; Chatham; Cherokee; Chowan; Clay; Cleveland; Columbus; Craven; Cumberland; Currituck; Dare; Davidson; Davie; Duplin; Durham; Edgecombe; Forsyth; Franklin; Gaston; Gates; Graham; Granville; Greene; Guilford; Halifax; Harnett; Haywood; Henderson; Hertford; Hoke; Hyde; Iredell; Jackson; Johnston; Jones; Lee; Lenoir; Lincoln; Macon; Madison; Martin; McDowell; Mecklenburg; Mitchell; Montgomery; Moore; Nash; New Hanover; Northampton; Onslow; Orange; Pamlico; Pasquotank; Pender; Perquimans; Person; Pitt; Polk; Randolph; Richmond; Robeson; Rockingham; Rowan; Rutherford; Sampson; Scotland; Stanly; Stokes; Surry; Swain; Transylvania; Tyrrell; Union; Vance; Wake; Warren; Washington; Watauga; Wayne; Wilkes; Wilson; Yadkin; Yancey.\"],\r\n",
      "        BBOX[33.83,-84.33,36.59,-75.38]],\r\n",
      "    ID[\"EPSG\",32119]]\r\n",
      "Data axis to CRS axis mapping: 1,2\r\n",
      "id: String (0.0)\r\n",
      "OGRFeature(clip):0\r\n",
      "  id (String) = (null)\r\n",
      "  POLYGON ((15.24 38.09,15.32 38.09,15.32 38.0,15.24 38.0,15.24 38.09))\r\n",
      "\r\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "! ogrinfo -al /tmp/clip.json"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 139,
   "id": "f05d67ff",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import rasterio.mask\n",
    "import fiona"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 140,
   "id": "ff1b3c67",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "[{'type': 'Polygon',\n",
       "  'coordinates': [[(15.24, 38.09),\n",
       "    (15.32, 38.09),\n",
       "    (15.32, 38.0),\n",
       "    (15.24, 38.0),\n",
       "    (15.24, 38.09)]]}]"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 140,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "with fiona.open('/tmp/clip.json', 'r') as geojson:\n",
    "    shapes = [feature['geometry'] for feature in geojson]\n",
    "shapes"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 141,
   "id": "0b064cc3",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "(8, 360, 320)"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 141,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "clip_image, clip_transform = rasterio.mask.mask(multi, shapes, crop=True) # why transform? ;-)\n",
    "clip_image.shape"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 142,
   "id": "26e305cb",
   "metadata": {
    "scrolled": true
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "{'driver': 'GTiff',\n",
       " 'dtype': 'uint16',\n",
       " 'nodata': None,\n",
       " 'width': 400,\n",
       " 'height': 440,\n",
       " 'count': 8,\n",
       " 'crs': CRS.from_epsg(4326),\n",
       " 'transform': Affine(0.00025, 0.0, 15.24,\n",
       "        0.0, -0.00025, 38.11)}"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 142,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "multi.meta"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 143,
   "id": "f0ab4eb5",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "{'driver': 'GTiff',\n",
       " 'dtype': 'uint16',\n",
       " 'nodata': None,\n",
       " 'width': 360,\n",
       " 'height': 320,\n",
       " 'count': 8,\n",
       " 'crs': CRS.from_epsg(4326),\n",
       " 'transform': Affine(0.00025, 0.0, 15.24,\n",
       "        0.0, -0.00025, 38.089999999999996)}"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 143,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "profile = multi.meta\n",
    "profile.update({'driver': 'GTiff', \n",
    "                'width': clip_image.shape[1], \n",
    "                'height': clip_image.shape[2], \n",
    "                'transform': clip_transform,\n",
    "               })\n",
    "profile"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 144,
   "id": "daf635da",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "dst = rasterio.open('/tmp/clipped.tif', 'w+', **profile)\n",
    "dst.write(clip_image)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 145,
   "id": "27966fb6",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "(320, 360)"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 145,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "dst.shape"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 146,
   "id": "a2ac46e4",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "(8, 320, 360)"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 146,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "rst = dst.read(masked=True)\n",
    "rst.shape"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 147,
   "id": "880fc8a0",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "\n",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 432x288 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {
      "needs_background": "light"
     },
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "<AxesSubplot:>"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 147,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "rasterio.plot.show(rst[0])"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "f1721bcc",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Note that all read/write operation in RasterIO are performed for the whole size of the dataset, so the package is limited (somehow ...) by memory available. An alternative is using *windowing* in read/write operation."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 148,
   "id": "75bee51f",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "Window(col_off=0, row_off=10, width=100, height=100)"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 148,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import rasterio.windows\n",
    "\n",
    "rasterio.windows.Window(0,10,100,100)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 149,
   "id": "50f4e695",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "(8, 100, 100)"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 149,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "win = rasterio.windows.Window(0,10,100,100)\n",
    "src = rasterio.open('/tmp/clipped.tif', mode='r')\n",
    "w = src.read(window=win)\n",
    "w.shape"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "fcf31344",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Of course if you tile the input source and output sub-windows of results, a new trasform will need to be provided for each tile, and the `window_transform()` method can be used for that."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 150,
   "id": "52d44360",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "Affine(0.00025, 0.0, 15.24,\n",
       "       0.0, -0.00025, 38.089999999999996)"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 150,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "src.transform"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 151,
   "id": "f5c80b37",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "Affine(0.00025, 0.0, 15.24,\n",
       "       0.0, -0.00025, 38.0875)"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 151,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "src.window_transform(win)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "7173202e",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Note that in many cases could be convenient aligning to the existing (format related) block size organization of the file, which *should* be the minimal chunck of I/O in GDAL."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 152,
   "id": "9b9a4f18",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "1 (1, 360)\n",
      "2 (1, 360)\n",
      "3 (1, 360)\n",
      "4 (1, 360)\n",
      "5 (1, 360)\n",
      "6 (1, 360)\n",
      "7 (1, 360)\n",
      "8 (1, 360)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "for i, shape in enumerate(src.block_shapes, 1):\n",
    "    print(i, shape)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "e8cd21cd",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "More information, examples and documentation about `RasterIO` are [here](https://rasterio.readthedocs.io/en/stable/index.html)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "73b7214b",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    " ## 1.3 The rio CLI tool \n",
    "\n",
    "RasterIO has also a command line tool (`rio`) which can be used to perform a series of operation on rasters, which complementary in respect with the GDAL tools.If you install rasterio from distribution/kit it will reside in the ordinary system paths (note: you could find a `rasterio` binary instead of `rio`). If you install from the PyPI repository via `pip` it will install under `~/.local/bin`."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 153,
   "id": "5e82fd3e",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Usage: rio [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...\r\n",
      "\r\n",
      "  Rasterio command line interface.\r\n",
      "\r\n",
      "Options:\r\n",
      "  -v, --verbose           Increase verbosity.\r\n",
      "  -q, --quiet             Decrease verbosity.\r\n",
      "  --aws-profile TEXT      Select a profile from the AWS credentials file\r\n",
      "  --aws-no-sign-requests  Make requests anonymously\r\n",
      "  --aws-requester-pays    Requester pays data transfer costs\r\n",
      "  --version               Show the version and exit.\r\n",
      "  --gdal-version\r\n",
      "  --show-versions         Show dependency versions\r\n",
      "  --help                  Show this message and exit.\r\n",
      "\r\n",
      "Commands:\r\n",
      "  blocks     Write dataset blocks as GeoJSON features.\r\n",
      "  bounds     Write bounding boxes to stdout as GeoJSON.\r\n",
      "  calc       Raster data calculator.\r\n",
      "  clip       Clip a raster to given bounds.\r\n",
      "  convert    Copy and convert raster dataset.\r\n",
      "  edit-info  Edit dataset metadata.\r\n",
      "  env        Print information about the Rasterio environment.\r\n",
      "  gcps       Print ground control points as GeoJSON.\r\n",
      "  info       Print information about a data file.\r\n",
      "  insp       Open a data file and start an interpreter.\r\n",
      "  mask       Mask in raster using features.\r\n",
      "  merge      Merge a stack of raster datasets.\r\n",
      "  overview   Construct overviews in an existing dataset.\r\n",
      "  rasterize  Rasterize features.\r\n",
      "  rm         Delete a dataset.\r\n",
      "  sample     Sample a dataset.\r\n",
      "  shapes     Write shapes extracted from bands or masks.\r\n",
      "  stack      Stack a number of bands into a multiband dataset.\r\n",
      "  transform  Transform coordinates.\r\n",
      "  warp       Warp a raster dataset.\r\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "! rio --help"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 154,
   "id": "a6edb09c",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "{\r\n",
      " \"bbox\": [\r\n",
      "  -83.0,\r\n",
      "  -56.0,\r\n",
      "  -34.0,\r\n",
      "  14.0\r\n",
      " ],\r\n",
      " \"geometry\": {\r\n",
      "  \"coordinates\": [\r\n",
      "   [\r\n",
      "    [\r\n",
      "     -83.0,\r\n",
      "     -56.0\r\n",
      "    ],\r\n",
      "    [\r\n",
      "     -34.0,\r\n",
      "     -56.0\r\n",
      "    ],\r\n",
      "    [\r\n",
      "     -34.0,\r\n",
      "     14.0\r\n",
      "    ],\r\n",
      "    [\r\n",
      "     -83.0,\r\n",
      "     14.0\r\n",
      "    ],\r\n",
      "    [\r\n",
      "     -83.0,\r\n",
      "     -56.0\r\n",
      "    ]\r\n",
      "   ]\r\n",
      "  ],\r\n",
      "  \"type\": \"Polygon\"\r\n",
      " },\r\n",
      " \"properties\": {\r\n",
      "  \"filename\": \"SA_elevation_mn_GMTED2010_mn_msk.tif\",\r\n",
      "  \"id\": \"0\",\r\n",
      "  \"title\": \"geodata/dem/SA_elevation_mn_GMTED2010_mn_msk.tif\"\r\n",
      " },\r\n",
      " \"type\": \"Feature\"\r\n",
      "}\r\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "! rio bounds --indent 1 geodata/dem/SA_elevation_mn_GMTED2010_mn_msk.tif # output a GeoJSON polygon for the bounding box"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "9320b2cd",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "A quite interesting feature is the raster calculator which uses a Lisp-like language notation, which is the s-expression notation of *Snuggs*, part of `numpy`. Largely undocumented, you need to have a look to the code\n",
    "[here](https://github.com/mapbox/snuggs/blob/master/snuggs/__init__.py) and its README file :-("
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 155,
   "id": "e94d3f33",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "! rio calc \"(+ 2.0 (* 0.95 (read 1)))\" --overwrite geodata/dem/SA_elevation_mn_GMTED2010_mn_msk.tif /tmp/elev.tif # Lisp like lists :-( "
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "8a14611e",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Simple comuputations can be done via `rio` or written via ad hoc script at your will. "
   ]
  }
 ],
